title: Advancing the Southwest Safety Net Embedded Scientist Research and Training (SSNE-STaR) Center
authors_full: Roshini Moodley Naidoo MBChB, MBA, MPH, Matthew Martin PhD, Lauren Baker MS, Marisa Domino PhD, Punam Ohri-Vachaspati, PhD, Adela Grando, PhD, Anita Murcko, MD, William Riley, PhD

summary.summary_30s: This poster describes SSNE-STaR, an AHRQ/PCORI-funded Learning Health System Center designed to build embedded CER/PCOR capacity within safety net organizations serving medically underserved populations.

talktrack_2m.what_this_is_about: This poster describes SSNE-STaR, an AHRQ/PCORI-funded Learning Health System Center designed to build embedded CER/PCOR capacity within safety net organizations serving...
talktrack_2m.what_they_did: Program description / training program report with early outcomes (Learning Health System Center).. 18 enrolled scholars; 16 active/embedded (Arizona)
talktrack_2m.what_they_found: Since launch, SSNE-STaR enrolled 18 scholars, with 16 currently active and embedded across Arizona safety net health systems.
talktrack_2m.why_it_matters: A structured, mentored embedded scientist model can expand CER/PCOR capacity within safety net systems.
talktrack_2m.one_smart_follow_up: How do you define an “embedded scholar” in SSNE-STaR—what portion of time is embedded in the clinical system versus academic work?

key_numbers: “Cohort: Enrollment Chart (2024–2026)” and “Scholar Research Development Pipeline (Aug 2024–Mar 2026)”.
18 scholars enrolled; 16 active and embedded across Arizona safety net systems.
Year-2 Rapid Implementers support dissemination and implementation.
takeaways: Since launch, SSNE-STaR enrolled 18 scholars, with 16 currently active and embedded across Arizona safety net health systems.
Enrollment exceeded the original 12-scholar target, suggesting strong demand for structured CER/PCOR training within clinical settings.
Scholars represent major partner organizations including Banner Health, Valleywise Health, District Medical Group, Phoenix Children’s Hospital, and Barrow Neurological...

general: What is the main takeaway you hope attendees remember from this poster?

Why does this work matter beyond this specific poster session?
undergrad: What part of this project would be most helpful for an undergraduate student to learn from?
What skills or tools were most important for getting this project done?
If an undergraduate wanted to get involved in work like this, where would you suggest they start?
grad: How do you define an “embedded scholar” in SSNE-STaR—what portion of time is embedded in the clinical system versus academic work?
What was the key design or analysis tradeoff in this project?
If you were extending this into a next study, what would you tighten or measure differently?
researcher: How do you define an “embedded scholar” in SSNE-STaR—what portion of time is embedded in the clinical system versus academic work?
What distinguishes the three cores (Administration, REC, RDAC) in day-to-day support, and what does a typical month look like for a scholar across these cores?
What CER/PCOR competencies are explicitly taught in the Research Education Core, and how are they assessed?
clinician: How could these findings affect clinical care, screening, or decision-making in practice?
What kind of patient or care setting would this poster be most relevant for?
What would need to happen before this work would be ready for broader clinical use?
community: Who in the community is most likely to benefit if this work moves forward?
What kinds of community partnerships would strengthen the next phase of this project?
What would meaningful real-world impact look like for the people touched by this work?
leader: What type of support, infrastructure, or investment would help this work move further?
